title: Pod as quadlet service hangs after a couple of days
body:
### Issue Description

I have a Pod definition in a yaml file, I have a .kube file to run the Pod. The pod starts fine but after a couple of days the containers are found in "Created" state and they can't be started again.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. create a custom pod file with one container inside
2. create a .kube file:
```
[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

[Unit]

[Kube]
Yaml=/home/podman/.config/containers/systemd/mypod.yml

[Service]
Restart=always

Environment=<some vars>
Environment=<some vars>
```
3. start the service: `systemctl --user start mypod`
4. check containers: `podman container ls --all` . They are running.
5. A couple of days later check containers again: they are in "Created" state.

### Describe the results you received

The containers are in "Created" state.

The issue doesn't always appear the same.
Sometimes the containers are in reality running, but showing as "Created". Sometimes they are stuck.

I have seen errors like this:
```
Failed to open cgroups file: /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/...memory.events
"Checking if infra needs to be stopped: removing pod fdf336711fa9137a5a81090d788e27231f33dff482956676fa2bab7d522059a5 cgroup: Unit user-libpod_pod_fdf336711fa9137a5a81090d788e27231f33dff482956676fa2bab7d522059a5.slice not loaded."
```
at another occasion these errors (when restarting the systemd service):
```
Pods stopped:
Pods removed:
Secrets removed:
Volumes removed:
[starting container ef8d3eb4df708ba22aaf71fc7149481220492c8c1d41c737f162c7befc49503a: failed to mount runtime directory for rootless netns: no such file or directory]
[starting container ef8d3eb4df708ba22aaf71fc7149481220492c8c1d41c737f162c7befc49503a: failed to mount runtime directory for rootless netns: no such file or directory starting container 92837f50c8e7fcb738172a4055bf1dcbfd140117828b3e1e6e73aaf576003628: a dependency of container 92837f50c8e7fcb738172a4055bf1dcbfd140117828b3e1e6e73aaf576003628 failed to start: container state improper]
Pod:
a31d4bd13a4d47c43e83ea6cfdc9f7039fe998c6a2d607a1e9332879739a55d3
Container:
92837f50c8e7fcb738172a4055bf1dcbfd140117828b3e1e6e73aaf576003628
starting container ef8d3eb4df708ba22aaf71fc7149481220492c8c1d41c737f162c7befc49503a: failed to mount runtime directory for rootless netns: no such file or directory
starting container 92837f50c8e7fcb738172a4055bf1dcbfd140117828b3e1e6e73aaf576003628: a dependency of container 92837f50c8e7fcb738172a4055bf1dcbfd140117828b3e1e6e73aaf576003628 failed to start: container state improper
Error: failed to start 2 containers
```

### Describe the results you expected

The pod and its containers should run indefinitely.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: We only support the latest version upstream, please verify with podman 5.5. There have been many bugfixes since then for example the "rootless netns" code has been completely rewritten.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
